sentence: Subjective:

The patient is a 34-year-old female with a history of chronic migraines and hypertension who presents with abdominal pain that started 10 days ago with mild discomfort on her right side, which has worsened over the last couple of days.
The pain is a 4 or 5 out of 10 in severity, but can reach up to 7 or 8.
The patient reports that the pain worsens after meals, is localized to the right side, and radiates slightly to the left.
She also experiences nausea, heartburn, and fatigue.
The patient denies fever, urinary symptoms, diarrhea, or blood in her stool.
She has not had any unusual travel or exposures.
The patient's migraines have been rare and responsive to Excedrin, but she occasionally takes sumatriptan for breakthrough migraines.
Past medical history:
The patient has a history of chronic migraines and hypertension.
She has been taking lisinopril 20 milligrams per day for hypertension.
Review of systems:
The patient reports nausea, heartburn, and fatigue.
Current medications:
Excedrin as needed for migraines
Sumatriptan as needed for breakthrough migraines
Lisinopril 20 milligrams per day for hypertension
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure is elevated today, but the exact values are not specified.
Physical exam: The patient has a grade 3 out of 6 systolic ejection murmur on her heart exam, which is unchanged from prior exams.
The cardiovascular exam is otherwise normal.
The abdominal exam reveals mild to moderate tenderness in the right upper quadrant, with an equivocal Murphy's sign.
Test Results:
Not mentioned
Assessment / Problem List:
The patient has acute abdominal pain with right upper quadrant tenderness, possibly due to gallstones or an infection in the gallbladder.
The patient has a history of chronic migraines, and her migraines are well-managed with Excedrin and sumatriptan.
The patient's hypertension is under adequate control on lisinopril.
Problem list: Acute abdominal pain; Chronic migraines; Hypertension
Plan:
I will order blood tests (CBC, chem 12, urinalysis, pregnancy test) and an ultrasound of the right upper quadrant to investigate the cause of the patient's abdominal pain.
I will also prescribe a refill of lisinopril 20 milligrams per day for hypertension.
I will write a prescription for sumatriptan for the patient's migraines.
I will instruct the patient not to eat anything until the results of the tests are available.
Follow-up:
I will follow up on the results of the tests today and check on the patient's condition.
I will see the patient in about four weeks to reassess her condition.
